content: FastVideo is a modular framework that separates models (encoders, VAEs, transformers) from execution logic (pipelines and stages)
Pipelines orchestrate execution by organizing multiple stages such as text encoding, denoising, and decoding
ForwardBatch is the shared state object passed between pipeline stages containing inputs, intermediate states, and outputs
Transformer diffusion models are implemented in fastvideo/models/dits
VAE implementations are located in fastvideo/models/vaes
Text and image encoders are implemented in fastvideo/models/encoders
FastVideoArgs is the central configuration system controlling model loading, inference settings, and distributed execution
Video generation is performed through the VideoGenerator API which handles model loading and pipeline execution
The denoising stage is the core computation loop that iteratively transforms noise into video frames
FastVideo supports distributed execution using multi-GPU workers coordinated by an executor system
FastVideo uses a pipeline abstraction where ComposedPipelineBase manages model loading, stage orchestration, and data flow
Each pipeline stage implements a forward(batch, args) interface and modifies the ForwardBatch state
Pipeline stages include input validation, text encoding, latent preparation, denoising, and decoding
Schedulers control diffusion steps and convert predicted noise into updated latent representations
FastVideo supports tensor parallelism and sequence parallelism for distributed model execution
Attention backends in FastVideo include FlashAttention, sliding tile attention, and sparse attention implementations
Pipeline implementations are located in fastvideo/pipelines including subdirectories like basic, preprocess, and training
CLI entrypoints are implemented in fastvideo/entrypoints/cli for commands like generate, serve, and benchmark
FastVideo uses an executor-worker system where each GPU runs a worker process coordinated by a central executor
Platform abstraction allows FastVideo to support different hardware backends such as CUDA and other accelerators
To install FastVideo from source: clone the repository and run uv pip install -e .
Recommended setup uses uv: uv venv --python 3.12 and uv pip install fastvideo
FastVideo requires Python 3.10–3.12 and CUDA 11.8+ for GPU setups
Run all tests using pytest tests/ or pytest fastvideo/tests/
Run pre-commit on all files using pre-commit run --all-files
Use VideoGenerator.from_pretrained() to load models and generate videos
Basic inference is done by calling generator.generate_video(prompt, ...)
Wan pipelines are implemented in fastvideo/pipelines/basic/wan
VideoGenerator is defined in fastvideo/entrypoints/video_generator.py
To run a basic inference: create a VideoGenerator with from_pretrained(), then call generator.generate_video(prompt)
To run the Gradio demo: navigate to examples/inference/gradio and run the serving or local demo script
Finetuning scripts are located in examples/training/finetune and scripts/finetune
Dataset preparation scripts are located in scripts/dataset_preparation and examples/dataset
Configuration files are stored in fastvideo/configs
Dataset loaders are implemented in fastvideo/dataset and fastvideo/dataset/dataloader
FastVideo uses a registry system to dynamically register models, pipelines, and components for extensibility
